title: Scalable shared-prefix hybrid scorer

queries: candidate aware multiclass scorer scalable shared prefix autoregressive rollout replay confirmed hybrid signal
autocomplete multiclass shared prefix candidate token scoring pairwise interaction non exhaustive stage3 redesign

evidence.why_now: EXP093 established that candidate-conditioned interaction carries real signal, and EXP096 plus EXP099 established that adding candidate-token autoregressive signal creates the first replay-confirmed representative-task bundle. EXP105 and the architecture review then retired the current exhaustive hybrid head because it is not the acceptable long-term scalable path.

hypothesis: Keep the replay-confirmed hybrid family but replace exhaustive per-candidate autoregressive rollout with a shared-prefix candidate lattice over class-id token sequences. Pairwise candidate interaction stays as the candidate-aware signal, while prefix-shared decoding preserves candidate-token evidence with non-multiplicative scaling in prompt length and class count.
notes: This candidate is finetune-only and uses the standard three-task Stage 3 subset bundle.
The design goal is to keep the replay-confirmed hybrid signal while replacing exhaustive per-candidate decoding with shared-prefix scoring.
Launched on 2026-05-08 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Resolved idle-GPU placement at launch time: user-churn -> lab25211 GPU 0, user-ltv -> lab25211 GPU 5, item-incoterms -> lab25211 GPU 6.
First orchestrator status check after launch showed all three windows up and logs in DB-load startup.
Completed on 2026-05-08 with global verdict: failed.
Outcome: user-churn improved clearly, item-incoterms was only neutral within the strict delta threshold, and user-ltv regressed below the strict baseline.
